references.text: Backer, CA. 1951. Flagellariaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 4: 245-248
Wepfer, PH & HP Linder. 2014. The taxonomy of Flagellaria. Australian Systematic Botany 27: 159-179.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 25 January 2026

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 333 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 191; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 246; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific biodiv. 17 (2024) 11; Raganas et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 1194; Type: from Java. var. bifurcata Wepfer & H.P.Linder, Austral. Syst. Bot. 27 (2014) 173; Type: from Indonesia. Native. var. indica. = Flagellaria philippinensis Elmer, LPB 1 (1908) 274; Type: Elmer 9142 (K, L, Z, iso), Luzon: Tayabas, Lucban, May-1907. Distribution: CEBU, LUZON: Quezon. Native. var. minor (Blume) Hook.f., Fl. Brit. India 6 (1892) 391; ≡ Flagellaria minor Blume, Syst. Veg., 15th Ed. 7 (1830) 1493; Type: from Indonesia. Native.
species.distribution: Africa, Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Indian Ocean, Japan, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madagascar, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. APO, APULIT, BALABAC, BASILAN, BATAN, BILIRAN, BORACAY (photos), BUYAYAO (photos), CAMIGUIN, CAMIGUIN DE BABUYANES, CATANDUANES, CEBU, CULION, DINAGAT, GUIMARAS, HOMONHON, LEYTE, LUBANG, LUZON: Albay, Apayao (photos), Aurora, Bataan, Batangas, Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, La Union, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Tarlac, Zambales, MASBATE (photos), MINASAWA, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao, Davao Oriental (photos), Davao del Norte, Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, Maguindanao del Norte, Misamis Oriental (photos), Surigao del Norte, Zamboanga Sibugay, MINDORO, PALAUI (photos), PALAWAN, PANAY, POLILLO, SAMAR, SEMIRARA, SIARGAO, SIASI, SIBUTU, SIBUYAN, TAWI-TAWI.
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines in most islands and provinces. Common in secondary forests. 0-1500m.
